  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 85,

  "confidence": 0.95,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Drywall panels installed tight to the floor with no gap",
    "Risk of moisture wicking from the subfloor or slab",
    "Lack of allowance for structural deflection and settling"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut or trim the bottom edge of the drywall panels to establish a 3/8 inch to 1/2 inch gap above the floor line",
    "Verify all bottom plates are clear of debris before re-fastening if panels must be re-hung"
  ],
  "rationale": "Installing gypsum board tight to the floor violates GA-216 and standard trade practices. A minimum gap of 3/8 to 1/2 inch is required to prevent moisture absorption from the floor and to allow for structural movement/deflection without cracking the drywall.",
  "references": [
    "GA-216 Section 4.6",
    "IRC Chapter 7"
  ]
}
